data.title: Turners Automotive Group Ltd.: Two-Speed Economy is Expected to Persist into Calendar Year 2026
Red Post Energy Announces Madhivanan T. A. ("Madhi") as Advisory Board Member, Strengthening Global Infrastructure & Data-Center Expertise
data.description:
HOUSTON, Nov. 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Red Post Energy is proud to announce the appointment of Madhivanan T. A. ("Madhi") to its Advisory Board. A strategic global leader with more than 30 years of experience across energy, chemicals, oil & gas, automotive, and technology sectors, Madhi brings unparalleled expertise in building resilient, scalable, and high-performance infrastructure worldwide.

In his current role as Director of Global Supply Chain at Microsoft, Madhi leads mission-critical data-center infrastructure delivery across the Eastern United States and oversees power-infrastructure execution throughout Latin America. He manages multi-billion-dollar capital programs that underpin the digital economy, ensuring accelerated timelines, capital efficiency, and robust supplier ecosystems that support global scaling efforts.

Madhi is recognized for his disciplined execution of Giga Projects, mastery of long-lead global supply chains, and commitment to developing diverse, high-performing teams. His work ensures that the world's largest technology platforms have the stable, reliable power and operational resilience they need to meet soaring data, AI, and cloud-infrastructure demands.

Prior to Microsoft, Madhi spent more than a decade at DOW Inc., where he delivered mega-projects and turnaround programs across Europe, LATAM, Asia, and the Middle East, strengthening global capital execution, scenario planning, and operational risk management. His earlier leadership roles at Reliance Industries, Maruti Suzuki, and Honda further expanded his operational and manufacturing depth across multiple continents.

A PMP-certified executive, ICF-certified coach, and Harvard-trained board leader, Madhi blends rigorous governance with a thoughtful, people-centered leadership philosophy. His global perspective and strategic insight align directly with Red Post Energy's mission of building long-life, high-capacity power infrastructure that supports both national energy security and the growing demands of next-generation technologies.

"Madhi's experience leading global supply chains for one of the world's most influential technology companies brings invaluable perspective to Red Post Energy," said Lance Medlin, President & Founder of Red Post Energy. "His understanding of complex capital execution, power-infrastructure delivery, and AI-driven data-center growth will help guide our strategy as we build the energy backbone of America's future."

Madhi's appointment reflects Red Post Energy's commitment to assembling top-tier, globally experienced leadership to drive innovation in energy production, grid stability, data-center power solutions, and national-security-aligned infrastructure.

Media Contact

Lance Medlin

CEO & Founder

Red Post Energy

Media@redpostenergy.com

+1 (713) 575-8307

SOURCE Red Post Energy

data.title: CBOT soybeans extend retreat after Chinese buying sparked gains
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures closed lower on Wednesday as traders sought to gauge the strength of Chinese buying, following the trade truce between Washington and Beijing last month.

  • CBOT January soybeans (SF26) ended 17-1/4 cents lower at $11.36-1/4 per bushel.

  • CBOT December soymeal (SMZ25) closed down $8.10 at $318.90 per short ton, while December soyoil (BOZ25) slid 1.07 cent to 51.10 cents per pound.

  • The soybean market has retreated after rallying on Tuesday to its highest level since June 2024.

  • soy for shipment in December and January. Then, the U.S. soybeans to China on Wednesday.

  • Traders were waiting for the U.S. Department of Agriculture to release U.S. grain and soy export sales data on Thursday, after the agency halted the release of weekly reports during the federal government's shutdown.

  • For the week that ended on October 2, analysts estimated 2025-26 U.S. soybean export sales were 600,000 to 1.6 million metric tons, according to a Reuters poll.

data.title: ESALQ Cattle Analysis
data.description: cattle analysis CATTLE/CEPEA: Calf prices may continue to rise up to 26/27 transition Cepea, 19 - Price rises and price drops for calf throughout time end up defining what is known as the livestock cycle. Currently, calf prices are movingup, bringing questions about for how long they might continue on the rise. The CEPEA/ESALQ monthly calf price Index (Mato Grosso do Sul State) indicates that, from 2000 to October 2025 (values were deflated by the IGP-DI October/25),the periods of high prices last from 30 to 32 months. The last moment of price increases was from September/18 to April/21, when the Index rose 90% (in real terms). Current price rises for calf in Mato Grosso do Sul have been taking place since July 2024. Considering the historical data, the trend would be quotations moving up for more 13 or 15 months (totaling 30-32 months). Thus, the upward trend might occur up to late 2026. For the Portuguese version please refer to or click on <ESQ/CATL/ANALYS>

data.title: Clean Vision Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Clean Vision Corp, a company specializing in sustainable waste management solutions, has released its Q3 10-Q report, showcasing significant financial and operational developments. The report highlights the company's performance in Morocco and outlines future expansion plans, particularly in the United States.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $125,201, reflecting an increase from $107,946 in the prior year, indicating growth in sales from the company's operations in Morocco.
  • Gross Margin: $105,900, showing an improvement from $96,682, suggesting better cost management and efficiency in operations.
  • Loss from Operations: $(4,174,916), compared to $(3,235,749) in the previous year, indicating increased operating expenses.
  • Net Loss: $(5,633,750), slightly improved from $(5,705,277) in the prior year, reflecting ongoing challenges in achieving profitability.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $(0.01), consistent with the previous year, indicating no change in earnings per share despite operational challenges.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company's operations in Morocco generated approximately $125,000 in revenue from the sale of commodities, specifically from the provision of pyrolysis services and the sale of byproducts. This marks an increase from the previous year's revenue of $108,000 from the same operations.
  • Geographical Performance: The company's operations in Morocco have been a focal point, with the pyrolysis facility in Agadir having a capacity to convert 20 tons per day of waste plastic. The company is also planning to expand its operations in West Virginia, USA, with a facility expected to be operational in Q4 2025.
  • Sales Units: The Morocco facility is currently converting 20 tons per day of waste plastic through pyrolysis, showcasing the company's operational capacity in this region.
  • New Production Launches: Clean-Seas West Virginia is expected to begin operations in Q4 2025, initially converting 50 tons per day of plastic feedstock, with plans to expand to over 500 tons per day within three years.
  • Future Outlook: The company is focused on expanding its operations and increasing its production capacity. The West Virginia facility is expected to play a significant role in this expansion, with plans to scale up operations significantly over the next few years.

SEC Filing: Clean Vision Corp [ CLNV ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Arizona regulators approve doc-less intervention in DOE, FERC large loads docket
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/324 pm EST/2024 GMT **FERC required to take final action by April 30 **NARUC urges preservation of state authority The Arizona Corporation Commission agreed Nov. 19 to a document-less intervention related to a US Department of Energy letter to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to assert jurisdiction over the interconnection of large loads. The doc-less intervention allows the commission to show interest in the issue without filing a letter or comments into the docket. "It says, hey, we are interested in this issue," Commission Chair Kevin Thompson. "I think it's important that we stay abreast of this and understand what's going on. ... I do want to preserve our ability to have a seat at the table, and I just don't want to have to comment to FERC on any issues until we know the rules that we're playing by." All five commissioners agreed. "I think it's important that we do intervene," Commissioner Lea Marquez Peterson said. "This is such a hot controversial issue across the nation. It's important that Arizona has a voice in this conversation." DOE LETTER TO FERC The secretary's letter, which included a series of principles, requires FERC to consider the proposal and take final action by April 30, 2026. "This docket is basically to establish the principles and to begin the process to establish detailed interconnection requirements for large loads, which is something novel," Sean Bodkin, commission senior associate general counsel, said about the DOE's docket. "Loads have never been subject to jurisdiction in the past. It's always been generation and transmission. ... They're relying on the fact that this is an interconnected grid and that these large loads are relying on energy from possibly interstate commerce." In an Oct. 23 letter to FERC, the US Secretary of Energy proposed an advanced notice of proposed rulemaking on the interconnection of large loads to the interstate transmission system. "To usher in a new era of American prosperity, we must ensure all Americans and domestic industries have access to affordable, reliable and secure electricity," Secretary of Energy Chris Wright wrote in the letter to FERC. "To do this, large loads, including AI data centers, served by public utilities must be able to connect to the transmission system in a timely, orderly, and non-discriminatory manner. This is an urgent issue that requires prompt attention." While FERC historically has not exerted jurisdiction over load interconnections, "it is my view that the interconnection of large loads directly to the interstate transmission system to access the transmission system and the electricity transmitted over it falls squarely within the Commission's jurisdiction," Wright added. "Asserting Commission jurisdiction is in the public's interest." The National Association of Regulatory Utility Commissioners on Nov. 11 approved a resolution calling on FERC to preserve state retail authority when considering the proposed rule to greatly expand the federal government's role in the interconnection of data centers and other large loads. In separate actions, the commission approved certificates of environmental compatibility for the following: **West Valley Central Transmission Project **Harquahala North Transmission Line Project **Rio Sol To Saguaro Interconnect Project — Kassia Micek, kassia.micek@spglobal.com-- Edited by Giselle Rodriguez, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Goodman Property Trust 1H Net NZD61.8M
GE Vernova Shares Up After Taiwan Power Company Onshore Wind Deal
data.description:
By Terell Wright

Shares of GE Vernova rose after the company said it received its first international onshore wind repower upgrade agreement with Taiwan Power Company.

Shares climbed 6% to $587.53 and are up 73% year-to-date.

The power, wind and electrification company on Wednesday said it will provide Taiwan Power Company repower and upgrade kits for 25 GE Vernova turbines and deliver a five-year operations and maintenance services package.

GE Vernova said the project will modernize aging turbines, improving their performance and reliability, which will help the TPC produce more affordable, renewable power.

GE Vernova said it booked the order in the third quarter. Initial components are slated to be delivered this quarter, with retrofit installation taking place throughout 2026 and 2027.

Write to Terell Wright at terell.wright@wsj.com

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P 500 edge up ahead of Nvidia earnings; Dow down
data.description:
Key points:
  • Target drops after missing quarterly comparable sales estimates
  • Indexes slightly pare gains after Fed minutes
  • Indexes: Dow down 0.1%, S&P 500 up 0.2%, Nasdaq up 0.4%

By Caroline Valetkevitch

The S&P 500 and Nasdaq edged higher in volatile trading on Wednesday as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results, while the Dow eased.

Indexes pared gains after minutes from the last Federal Reserve meeting showed policymakers cautioned that lower borrowing costs could undermine the fight against inflation. The Fed cut rates by a quarter of a percentage point at each of its meetings in September and October.

Worries about a cooling labor market persisted ahead of Thursday's release of the September U.S. jobs report following the record-long U.S. government shutdown. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said it will not publish the October employment report, but will instead combine nonfarm payrolls for that month with November's report.

"A lot has changed since the Fed last met, but it seems as though the biggest disagreement among Fed officials was the timing of the next move, not the magnitude or direction," said Brian Jacobsen, chief economist at Annex Wealth Management in Menomonee Falls, Wisconsin.

Shares of Nvidia were up 1.7%, with its earnings seen as a test for the artificial intelligence-driven rally that has pushed the market to record highs this year.

The stock is "almost guaranteed to be volatile," following the report, said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York.

"When you're the biggest company out there, you're sort of in the headlights, and it often does not make sense how the stock reacts in the short term," he said.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average fell 53.77 points, or 0.12%, to 46,038.42, the S&P 500 gained 14.54 points, or 0.22%, to 6,631.86 and the Nasdaq Composite gained 95.36 points, or 0.42%, to 22,527.61.

Investors have worried about how companies will be able to make money from their huge investments in AI.

Options data from analytics firm Option Research & Technology Services (ORATS) showed an implied move of about 7% for Nvidia's stock in either direction after its results.

Among decliners, Target shares slipped after the retailer reported a bigger-than-expected drop in quarterly sales with cash-strapped U.S. consumers pulling back on discretionary spending.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 2.03-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, where there were 52 new highs and 186 new lows.

On the Nasdaq, 1,574 stocks rose and 3,006 fell as declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.91-to-1 ratio.

AFT Pharmaceuticals Ltd. 1H Net NZD4.7M
ICE Review: Canola Turns Weaker
data.description:
WINNIPEG, Manitoba--Intercontinental Exchange canola futures fell back on Wednesday, getting pressured by profit-taking and declines in comparable oils.

The Chicago soy complex was down hard while losses in MATIF rapeseed and Malaysian palm oil were more moderate. A downturn in crude oil was also felt in the vegetable oils.

Despite today's pullback, the January canola contract remained above its 20- and 50-day moving averages.

China not buying Canadian canola continued to hover in the background, exerting pressure.

A trader said he expects canola to fall to C$630-C$640 range and then slowly rebound upward.

The Canadian dollar retreated on Wednesday afternoon with the loonie sliding to 71.13 U.S. cents compared to Tuesday's close of 71.44.

There were 63,192 contracts traded on Wednesday, compared to 44,575 on Tuesday. Spreading accounted for 39,410 contracts traded.
